justification: Bird clearly identifies the contradiction and names all three conflicting sources (PM's BR-1, Finance's BR-2, Operations' BR-3). The escalation section is detailed and explicit. However, Bird produced acceptance criteria (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3) rather than refusing to do so, which violates the pass requirement of 'No acceptance criteria produced for the cancellation feature.' While AC-1 and AC-2 flag the contradiction, they are still structured acceptance criteria rather than a clean refusal to produce them. The response also provides a recommendation (Option B) which, while flagged as requiring stakeholder agreement, edges toward invented reconciliation. The formal escalation is present and strong, but the inclusion of acceptance criteria and a recommended resolution prevents a full pass.

observations.text: All three conflicting sources explicitly named and mapped to business rules BR-1, BR-2, BR-3
Escalation section clearly states the contradiction: BR-1 + BR-2 + BR-3 make cancellation simultaneously always-permitted and never-permitted
Confidence level set at 40, well below the 70 threshold, with clear reasoning about why the spec is internally inconsistent
Rejection reasons section explicitly states the three requirements are mutually exclusive
Produced three acceptance criteria (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3) despite the contradiction — pass requires no acceptance criteria for the cancellation feature
Provided a recommendation (Option B with credit notes) which, while hedged, moves toward reconciliation rather than pure escalation
Options are clearly labeled and presented as requiring stakeholder decision, not as engineering choices
Business impact analysis thoroughly covers the consequences of each possible resolution path
